prior_bugs_verified.useTimeTick_root_leak: FIXED — useTimeTick now scoped to StatsBar only, not called at App root
prior_bugs_verified.idle_dual_render: FIXED — idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkers.includes(w)) correctly excludes conference-seated workers from wander loop
prior_bugs_verified.synthetic_vibe_logs_destroyed_on_poll: FIXED — syncFromGitHub preserves e.source === 'synthetic' entries before merging fresh activity data

suggestions.title: Standup dual-render: working workers appear at desk AND at conference table simultaneously
suggestions.description: When standupActive is true (3+ workers, vibe 'crushing' or 'in-flow'), standupWorkers are rendered inside ConferenceTable as CharacterAvatars (Office.jsx line 1002-1018). But those same working workers are ALSO rendered as full Character components at their desks via the deskOccupants loop (lines 1546-1566). Carlos fixed the idle version of this in #69 but missed the working/standup case. A worker can visually appear twice simultaneously. Fix: skip standup workers from the desk Character render, or don't show avatars in the conference table for workers who are at active desks.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone effect: inner cleanup timeout is never cancelled on unmount
suggestions.description: In ProgressBar (App.jsx lines 213-219), when a milestone is crossed, an outer setTimeout (staggered per milestone) fires setFlashClass(), then schedules an inner setTimeout(() => setFlashClass(''), 600) to clear it. The outer timers are tracked in the 'timers' array and cleaned up on effect re-run. The inner timers are NOT tracked and NOT cancelled. If the component unmounts between the outer and inner firing (e.g., worker goes from working to idle mid-animation), the inner callback fires setFlashClass on an unmounted component. While React 18 silently ignores this, it is a state-leak pattern. Fix: store inner timer IDs in a ref and clear them in the cleanup.

suggestions.title: meetingWorkers array not memoized — huddle tracking useEffect fires every render
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx, meetingWorkers is computed as a plain variable (line 1200): 'const meetingWorkers = idleWorkers.length >= 2 ? idleWorkers.slice(...) : []'. This produces a new array reference on every render. The useEffect at line 1238 depends on [meetingWorkers], so it runs on EVERY render of the Office component — not just when meeting state actually changes. The lastHuddle.current ref guards against spurious updates internally, but the effect still runs needlessly. Fix: wrap meetingWorkers in useMemo with dependencies [idleWorkers]. Same applies to standupWorkers.

suggestions.title: Tabpanel elements missing tabIndex — keyboard users can't navigate into panel content
suggestions.description: The two role='tabpanel' divs (App.jsx lines 1264 and 1267) have no tabIndex attribute. Per ARIA best practice, visible tabpanels should have tabIndex={0} so keyboard users can Tab into the panel content after selecting a tab with arrow keys. Without it, focus goes straight past the panel content to the next interactive element in the DOM. This affects the entire tab navigation flow for screen reader and keyboard-only users.

suggestions.title: Manual sync (keyboard R + sync button) fires fetch with no AbortController
suggestions.description: The initial mount fetch (App.jsx line 876) correctly uses an AbortController to cancel in-flight requests on cleanup. However, the keyboard shortcut handler (line 861: syncFromGitHub()) and the sync button onClick (line 1199: syncFromGitHub()) both call syncFromGitHub without passing a signal. If a user hammers R repeatedly, or the component unmounts mid-request, these fetches cannot be cancelled. Each one can produce orphaned state updates. Fix: create and pass an AbortController signal in each call site, or manage a shared abort ref at the component level.

suggestions.title: quoteBonus footer tagline counter hard-caps at BONUS_TAGLINES.length — button dies silently
suggestions.description: In App.jsx line 1420, quoteBonus increments up to BONUS_TAGLINES.length (8) and then stops: 'p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p'. Once all 8 bonus taglines are exhausted, clicking the footer button does nothing. There's no cycling back to start and no visual indication the button has stopped working. The sparkle still shows, the cursor is still pointer, but it's dead. Fix: cycle back with p % BONUS_TAGLINES.length, or show a 'Start over' state, or just let it loop.

suggestions.title: Office floor right edge clips content on desktop — window element partially off-screen
suggestions.description: In the desktop screenshot, the right edge of the office floor is touching or slightly beyond the viewport boundary. The white/bright 'window' element at the right side appears to be partially clipped. The office floor container extends fully to the right edge with no breathing room. On smaller 1280px viewports (which is our test size), content at the right percentages (desks at left:57%, characters offset further) runs close to the boundary. Fix: either reduce the office floor's right-side percentage positions slightly, or ensure overflow:hidden with visible margins on the wrapper.

suggestions.title: getTeamVibe never returns 'after-hours' for an active team — time context ignored
suggestions.description: In vibe.js, getTeamVibe() only returns 'after-hours' when workers.length === 0. If any worker is active — even at 2am — the vibe will be 'crushing', 'in-flow', or 'slow-day'. The window element already reads actual clock time and renders a night sky. The WallClock renders actual time. But the vibe system is disconnected from time-of-day entirely. An on-fire incident at midnight looks identical to one at noon. Consider a secondary signal: if currentHour is outside business hours AND vibe would otherwise be 'slow-day', return 'after-hours' instead.

suggestions.title: Mobile: 'Office' tab label truncated at 375px viewport
suggestions.description: On the mobile screenshot at 375x812, the Office tab label shows as 'Offic' — the 'e' is cut. The tab-toggle has flex layout and both tabs share width, but the Office tab appears to lose its trailing character. This is likely a combination of the tab icon emoji (🏢) and text not fitting. Fix: reduce the tab font-size slightly on mobile, or ensure min-width on the tab-toggle, or abbreviate gracefully.

suggestions.title: handleTabKeyDown not memoized in App component
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 773 defines handleTabKeyDown as a plain function in the render body. It's passed as onKeyDown to the two tab buttons. Since it captures 'tab' state via closure and is re-created every render, it triggers no real bugs but violates the pattern of the rest of the file which consistently uses useCallback for event handlers. Wrap in useCallback([tab]) for consistency and to avoid unnecessary re-renders of the tab buttons.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter logic duplicated across three locations
suggestions.description: The activity filter predicate (type === 'hire', 'complete', 'error', 'system') is written out three separate times: once in the count display calculation (lines 1341-1347), once in the ActivityLog entries prop (lines 1398-1407), and conceptually in ActivityLog's own reverse/slice (lines 132-155). This means updating filter behavior requires touching multiple places. Extract to a named filterActivity(entries, typeFilter, workerFilter) function and call it from all three locations.

suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble overflows office floor boundary — not visible to screen readers
suggestions.description: In the desktop screenshot, Bloberto's speech bubble ('Night crew came to work. Respect.') renders above the office floor div's top boundary. It's a visually floating element. If the parent has overflow:hidden it would clip; if not, it bleeds into the tab bar area. The speech bubble has no aria-live region in the Office component context (there's an sr-only span at line 1306 for ping reactions, but not for Bloberto's ambient quotes). Bloberto's wisdom is invisible to screen readers. Add an aria-live='polite' region that mirrors Bloberto's current quote.

sofia_notes: Carlos actually delivered this time — three for three on the #69 bugs. useTimeTick moved to StatsBar (verified), idle dual-render correctly filtered (verified), synthetic vibe logs survive the poll (verified). Nice. That said, he left behind standup's identical twin problem for working workers — same class of bug, different status. The ProgressBar has a nested timeout that's just floating out there uncleaned. The meetingWorkers array getting a new reference every render is the kind of thing that seems harmless until the useEffect dependency comparison bites you. Accessibility score drops to 6 because the tabpanel tabIndex gap is a real keyboard navigation break, not cosmetic. The app still has excellent personality — the vibe sparklines, the idle micro-bubbles, the Bloberto quotes are all delightful. Fun factor stays at 9. But we are shipping subtle memory patterns and I have a spreadsheet.
